sourceMovie.title: Killers of the Flower Moon
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Killers of the Flower Moon (2023) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Martin Scorsese's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: In Cold Blood
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Killers of the Flower Moon clicked for you, In Cold Blood (1967) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and history, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Casino
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Killers of the Flower Moon clicked for you, Casino (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Killers of the Flower Moon?
faq.answer: Try In Cold Blood, Casino, Extremely Wicked, Shockingly Evil and Vile—each shares DNA with Killers of the Flower Moon while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Killers of the Flower Moon?
faq.answer: Killers of the Flower Moon (2023) is best described as Crime, History, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Killers of the Flower Moon worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
